title: Build GitHub User Search
mission: Understand side effects by building API-based UI.
description: Build a GitHub user search page that fetches public profile data from the GitHub REST API. Practice useEffect, async fetch, TypeScript API types, and loading/error/empty/success states.
requirements: Search input for GitHub username with controlled state
Submit search via button or Enter key — fetch on user action (not every keystroke yet)
Fetch https://api.github.com/users/{username} with fetch and async/await
Type the response with a GitHubUser TypeScript interface (login, avatar_url, html_url, name, bio, public_repos, etc.)
useEffect or async handler pattern — no fetch in unconditional render body
Loading state while request is in progress
Error state for 404, network failure, or non-ok response
Empty state before first search or when input is cleared
Success state showing avatar, name, login, bio, repo count, and link to profile
Reuse Day 03 input/button components where possible
acceptanceCriteria: Searching valid username (e.g. gaearon) shows profile card
Invalid username shows friendly error — not a blank screen
Empty state copy guides user to search
No infinite render loop from useEffect
npm run build passes with typed API data
hints: Define type GitHubUser matching fields you display — start with login, avatar_url, name, bio, public_repos, html_url.
async function searchUser() { setLoading(true); setError(null); try { ... } catch { ... } finally { setLoading(false); } }
Keep fetch in submit handler today; useEffect with username dep is valid if you prefer search-on-change — pick one pattern.

bonus.title: Request cleanup
bonus.description: Handle stale responses without debounce hook yet.
requirements: Use AbortController in useEffect cleanup when fetching on username change
Display last updated timestamp on success card
Add retry button on error state

debuggingScenarios.title: Infinite render
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Browser freezes; Network tab shows hundreds of requests
debuggingScenarios.cause: useEffect sets state on every run without correct dependencies or guard conditions
debuggingScenarios.fix: Add dependency array; only fetch when username non-empty; avoid setState that recreates deps every render

debuggingScenarios.title: Wrong dependency array
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Stale data shown after username changes, or effect never re-runs
debuggingScenarios.cause: Missing username in deps, or unstable object/function deps recreated each render
debuggingScenarios.fix: Include username in [username]; hoist fetch function or wrap in useCallback only if needed later

debuggingScenarios.title: Missing cleanup
debuggingScenarios.cause: No abort or cancelled flag when component unmounts or query changes
debuggingScenarios.fix: Return cleanup from useEffect that aborts fetch or sets ignore = true before setState
